action.reasoning: Observation: current heatmap model now runs end-to-end and yields valid predictions, but val/test grounding_score remains exactly 0.0 while mean IoU is tiny. Analysis: this is no longer a contract bug; the model is fundamentally not learning transferable GUI grounding. With 19 iterations and ample time left, the highest-upside move is to switch to the recommended stronger baseline: LoRA fine-tuning of the offline Qwen2.5-VL-3B model on a manageable subset, because the task is instruction-conditioned localization and the current from-scratch CNN lacks UI semantics. We should first implement a proper parser/utilities plus a minimal SFT training pipeline and run a small subset/1-epoch experiment to measure feasibility and get a real val score before scaling. Decision: add a Qwen LoRA training path, robust coordinate parser/decoder, and a matching predict.py that uses the fine-tuned VLM checkpoint; then run a short subset training and local val scoring. No eval submission yet until local val confirms nonzero improvement.
action.command: python train.py --train_subset 1000 --epochs 1 --batch_size 1 --grad_accum 4 --lr 1e-4 --out checkpoints/qwen_lora_small && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --output val_pred_qwen_small.json --limit 64 && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_small.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl
